zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis於1525年出生在英國英格蘭。他於1548年與Katherine Trye在英國格盧斯特斯爾普可萊其結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1559年9月17日在英國格盧斯特斯爾普可萊其享年34歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾普可萊其。

nameMeanings.name: Dennis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Dec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Andrzej, Casimir, Ignacy, Jerzy, Krystyna, Krzysztof, Malgorzata, Mikolaj, Stanislaw, Tadeusz.
Polish: from a pet form of the personal name Dionizy, Polish form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Dennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Denis (see Dennis ) + -son. Compare Tennyson .
English (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French deinzein, a burgess who enjoyed the privileges of those living deinz la cité ‘within the city’.

variants.name: Denny
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English Den(n)y, a pet form of the personal name Denis (see Dennis ). Compare Tenney .

English: habitational name from Danny in Hurstpierpoint, Sussex, recorded as Danye in 1343. Alternatively, the name may arise from Denny in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, but no medieval evidence with a habitative preposition has been found in the case of the latter.
Scottish: habitational name from Denny in Stirlingshire.

variants.name: Diniz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Augusto, Celso, Francisco, Luis, Ricardo, Rogerio, Alvaro, Anselmo, Antero, Carlos.  Portuguese Joao, Marcio.
Portuguese: from the personal name Dinis, a vernacular form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Denes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Laszlo, Miklos, Tibor, Aranka, Balint, Endre, Ferenc, Lajos, Zoltan.  Jewish Imrich, Meyer.  Romanian Doina, Matei, Radu.
Hungarian (Dénes): from the personal name Dénes, from Greek Dionysios (see Dennis ). The surname Dénes is also found in Slovakia; compare 2 below.
Slovak (Deneš and Déneš) and Romanian (also Deneş): from the Hungarian personal name Dénes ‘Dennis’, or an adaptation of the Hungarian surname Dénes (see 1 above).

variants.name: Dennett
meanings: English: from a pet form of Middle English Denis (see Dennis ), or possibly from a pet form of Denise, as, for example, Denote, a female character in Piers Plowman.

variants.name: Dinges
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dings .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Dings
meanings: Dutch and German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dinges .

variants.name: Dinis
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: from Dinis, a Portuguese personal name equivalent to Dennis .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis was born in 1525, in England, United Kingdom. He married Katherine Trye in 1548, in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 17 September 1559, in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 34, and was buried in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis est né en 1525, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Katherine Trye en 1548, à Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 17 septembre 1559, à Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterré à Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis wurde 1525 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1548 in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England Katherine Trye geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 17. September 1559 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis è nato il nel 1525 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Katherine Trye nel 1548, nel Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 17 settembre 1559, nel Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolto a Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennisは1525年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた。1548年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアプウックレチャーチでKatherine Tryeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1559年9月17日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアプウックレチャーチで34歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアプウックレチャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis(은)는 1525년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 1548년 영국 그루스스터실르 푸클렉후르치에서 Katherine Trye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1559년 9월 17일 영국 그루스스터실르 푸클렉후르치에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 푸클렉후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis nasceu em 1525, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Katherine Trye em 1548, em Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de setembro de 1559, em Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 34 anos, e foi sepultado em Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis родился в 1525 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Trye в 1548 г. в местности пукклечурч, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 17 сентября 1559 г. в местности пукклечурч, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 34 лет и был похоронен в местности пукклечурч, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh Dennis nació en 1525, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Katherine Trye en 1548, en Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 17 de septiembre de 1559, en Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultado en Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
